data:
Search results for "Scheme lexer token types boolean number identifier quote lambda":

Title: Formal Syntax of Scheme
URL: https://www.scheme.com/tspl3/grammar.html
Content: Programs and data are formed from tokens, whitespace, and comments. Tokens include identifiers, booleans, numbers, characters, strings, open and close

Title: Function for detecting quotes in Scheme code - Stack Overflow
URL: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/10472582/function-for-detecting-quotes-in-scheme-code
Content: All together, the 6sqv evaluation rule means that a quoted number or boolean evaluates to the number or boolean; the quote is simply discarded.

Title: Scheme - Lexical conventions
URL: https://www.cs.cmu.edu/Groups/AI/html/r4rs/r4rs_4.html
Content: Whitespace is used for improved readability and as necessary to separate tokens from each other, a token being an indivisible lexical unit such as an identifier

Title: A data-directed approach to lexing. | Lambda the Ultimate
URL: http://lambda-the-ultimate.org/node/5690
Content: The lexer uses a hash table. The table is keyed by an input character and optionally by the position in the token where the character is read.

Title: data types - What should be the datatype of the tokens a lexer returns to its parser? - Software Engineering Stack Exchange
URL: https://softwareengineering.stackexchange.com/questions/328636/what-should-be-the-datatype-of-the-tokens-a-lexer-returns-to-its-parser
Content: # What should be the datatype of the tokens a lexer returns to its parser? > In computer science, lexical analysis is the process of converting a sequence of characters (such as in a computer program or web page) into a sequence of tokens (**strings** with an identified "meaning"). Later on in the article He mentioned `Flex`, a already existing lexer, and said writing 'rules' with it would be simpler than writing a lexer by hand. If the lexer was going to return numbers, how would it read string literals? Wouldn't the lexer have to convert the string to binary numbers and then the parser would convert the numbers back to a string. It seems much more logical(and easier) for the lexer to return strings, and then let the parser convert any number string literals into actual numbers. To condense my question into a paragraph: When writing a lexer, and assuming that it could only return **one** data type(strings or numbers), which would be the more logical choice?
